surface.name: Esri mapping and location services getting started, with an ArcGIS Location Platform and JavaScript map candidate
surface.selection_basis: Esri's general mapping and location services page presents account, authentication, and client-API choices before a set of first-application tutorials. ArcGIS Location Platform and ArcGIS Maps SDK for JavaScript are retained as a complete candidate because each is listed first, not because Esri declares either a universal default.

alternatives_considered.name: ArcGIS Online account route
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: ArcGIS Online is a peer account type with a multi-user organization, organization-admin controls, and different billing ownership.

alternatives_considered.name: OAuth 2.0 user authentication
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: It is a peer authentication route for private applications and changes credential creation, user consent, and billing ownership.

alternatives_considered.name: Other Maps SDKs and open-source clients
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The Display a map tutorial is offered for multiple web and native SDKs without a universal client default.

entry_point.developer_intent: Follow Esri's general developer getting-started guidance to create credentials and display the first documented map.
assumed_prior_state: A browser, an accessible email inbox, and CodePen are available.
No ArcGIS account, subscription, organization, portal, API key, authentication choice, or client SDK is assumed.

documented_first_success.official_milestone: Display a map with the ArcGIS Basemap Styles service.
documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: In the JavaScript candidate, the CodePen application displays the topographic basemap for the Santa Monica Mountains and allows pan and zoom.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: Running the pen displays the topographic basemap centered near `-118.805, 34.020` at zoom 13 with the zoom component.
documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: The general page labels Display a map as a first-application tutorial, and the JavaScript tutorial ends its required steps by running the app and observing the named topographic map before listing later tutorials.

prerequisites.requirement: Choose an eligible ArcGIS account route; the candidate uses a free ArcGIS Location Platform account and its single-user organization.

For the email candidate, provide a unique email, compliant unique username and password, unique 3-to-16-character portal URL, and security question and answer, then accept the platform agreement and privacy policy.
Activate the account from the confirmation email within 24 hours.
Create API key credentials for a public application with Basemap styles service privilege and retain the one-time-visible API key.
Use a browser and a CodePen pen for the selected ArcGIS Maps SDK for JavaScript route.

primary_path.action: Open Esri's mapping and location services Get started page.
primary_path.output: Account, authentication, service, and client-API choices plus first-application tutorials are visible.
primary_path.success_signal: The page offers ArcGIS Location Platform and ArcGIS Online accounts, API key and OAuth routes, and multiple Display a map clients.

primary_path.action: Choose an account type, authentication method, client API, and first tutorial that fit the intended application.
details: The candidate below uses the first-listed ArcGIS Location Platform account, API key authentication for a public app, and ArcGIS Maps SDK for JavaScript.
primary_path.input: Application audience, account context, and client technology
primary_path.output: One route through account, credentials, and application execution is selected.
primary_path.success_signal: The corresponding account, credential, and tutorial pages are open.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The choice changes organization, payment responsibility, privileges, authentication, code, and completion path.

candidate_paths.name: ArcGIS Location Platform API key and JavaScript Display a map
candidate_paths.selection_basis: This combines the first-listed account and Display a map client with the tutorial's required API key route. It is a reproducible candidate, not a company-wide default.
candidate_paths.relationship_to_primary_path: It instantiates all four unresolved choices with one documented public-application route.

steps.action: Open ArcGIS Location Platform signup and complete the email form with the documented identity, portal URL, security question, agreement, and privacy inputs.
details: The email must not already belong to another Location Platform account.
The portal URL cannot be changed after account creation.
steps.input: Unique email, compliant username and password, unique portal URL, security answer, and legal acceptance
steps.success_signal: The signup form is complete and Esri sends activation instructions.
steps.failure_or_wait: Username and portal URL must be unique and meet the documented character rules.

steps.action: Follow the confirmation-email instructions within 24 hours.
steps.output: An activated ArcGIS Location Platform account and subscription
steps.success_signal: The account can sign in to the Location Platform dashboard.

steps.action: Go to `https://location.arcgis.com`, sign in, and click My portal from the dashboard.
Click Content > My content > New item, then Developer credentials > API key credentials > Next.
steps.output: The API key credential wizard opens.
steps.failure_or_wait: If credential options are absent, the account lacks the documented role or privileges.

steps.action: Choose Public application and click Next, then select No item access and click Next.
details: Private-item access is optional and not required for this basemap tutorial.
steps.output: A public credential without private item access
steps.failure_or_wait: A private application must use the corresponding private option instead.

steps.action: Select Location services > Basemaps, then click Next.
steps.output: Basemap styles service privilege is assigned.
steps.success_signal: The expiration and referrer screen opens.
steps.failure_or_wait: The map cannot access its basemap without the required privilege.

steps.action: Set the expiration date to one month from today, enter allowed referrer domains, and click Next.
details: The tutorial highly recommends referrer restrictions.
steps.output: Token lifetime and web-origin restrictions are configured.
steps.failure_or_wait: Changing expiration or referrers later invalidates the token.

steps.action: Set title `My API key credentials`, create or select folder `Developer credentials`, add tags and a description, click Next, review the summary, and click Next again.
steps.output: The API key credential item is saved.
steps.success_signal: The Generate API key window appears.
steps.failure_or_wait: Review privileges and item access before generating a key because they determine its reach.

steps.action: Select Generate the API key and go to item details page, confirm readiness to copy and save it, click Next, and copy the displayed key.
steps.output: A long-lived API key access token
steps.success_signal: The Copy your key window displays the token and it is retained for the application.
steps.failure_or_wait: The value is visible only once; losing it requires regeneration.

steps.action: Create a new CodePen pen and add the tutorial's basic HTML and full-page CSS.
steps.success_signal: The HTML body is ready for the map component.
steps.failure_or_wait: Outside CodePen, the document also needs complete HTML tags.

steps.action: Define global `esriConfig` before the SDK libraries and set its `apiKey` property to the copied access token.
steps.output: The application is configured to authenticate location-service requests.
steps.failure_or_wait: The token needs the Basemap privilege used by the tutorial.

steps.output: ArcGIS Maps SDK for JavaScript is loaded from Esri's CDN.
steps.success_signal: The custom map elements are available to the page.

steps.action: Add an `arcgis-map` component with basemap `arcgis/topographic`, center `-118.805, 34.020`, and zoom `13`, then add an `arcgis-zoom` component in the top-left slot.
steps.output: The map and zoom controls are declared.
steps.success_signal: The page contains the complete tutorial markup.
The Santa Monica Mountains basemap displays and can be panned and zoomed.
steps.failure_or_wait: No run recovery is documented in the selected tutorial.

branches.path: Use an organization account with the correct user type and role, then follow the ArcGIS Online variant of API key creation.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds organization-admin and role dependencies and changes which organization bears service usage.

branches.condition: Build a private app with signed-in users
branches.path: Create OAuth credentials and implement OAuth 2.0 user authentication instead of embedding an API key.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds redirect, user sign-in, consent, and token-flow work.

branches.condition: Choose another Display a map client
branches.path: Follow that SDK or open-source library's own Display a map tutorial.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Changes runtime prerequisites, project setup, code, and execution environment.

branches.condition: Choose an alternate Location Platform identity method
branches.path: Use an available organization-specific login route rather than the email candidate.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Introduces external identity and possible administrator or support dependencies; the same account page also contains conflicting statements about social signup availability for new accounts.

friction_gates.description: Account, authentication, and client choices materially change the route and no universal combination is selected.
Email signup requires unique account and portal identifiers, a security answer, legal acceptance, and email activation.
The key must have the Basemap styles service privilege.
The generated API key is visible only once and later regeneration invalidates the prior key.
Expiration and referrer restrictions are configured before generation, and changing them later invalidates the key.

time_to_first_success.value: The JavaScript Display a map tutorial is labeled Estimated time 10 minutes; Esri does not state that this includes account signup, activation, and API-key creation.

sources.title: Tutorial: Create an API key | Documentation | Esri Developer
sections_used: Set the expiration date and referrers

sources.title: Display a map | ArcGIS Maps SDK for JavaScript
sections_used: Add the ArcGIS Maps SDK for JavaScript script tag

uncertainties.question: Which account, authentication method, and client should define Esri Developer's company-wide first mile?
uncertainties.impact: The answer changes organization requirements, billing ownership, credential and consent work, runtime setup, and code.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The general page presents these as purpose-dependent peers; the candidate uses first-listed options rather than an official universal default.

uncertainties.question: Can a brand-new Location Platform user currently sign up with a social identity?
uncertainties.impact: The new-user identity path and verification steps differ.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The account page first says social signup is supported, but its Social media section says new social signup is not currently supported and limits it to migrated pre-June-2024 accounts.

excluded_after_success: Additional map layers, feature styling, geocoding, routing, data hosting, production deployment, license review, usage monitoring, billing configuration, API-key rotation, and OAuth implementation occur after the candidate map first displays.
